evals.prompt: Record the decision to use zoneinfo over pytz for timezone handling
evals.expected_output: ADR file written with correct sequential numbering. Contains Decision, Rationale, and Consequences sections. Status accepted. Captures the alternatives considered.
assertions: ADR number is sequential — one higher than the highest existing ADR
ADR frontmatter has id field matching the filename number
ADR contains a ## Decision section with a single clear decision statement
ADR contains a ## Rationale section explaining why this option was chosen
ADR contains a ## Consequences section describing what this decision enables or constrains
ADR mentions at least one alternative that was considered and rejected
ADR does not record a trivial decision — it represents a real fork in the road

evals.prompt: Record the decision to use TOML for config files
evals.expected_output: ADR captures the decision with alternatives (JSON, YAML, INI) and the specific rationale for choosing TOML.
assertions: ADR ## Decision states which format was chosen
ADR ## Rationale explains why TOML was chosen over alternatives
ADR names at least one alternative format that was considered
ADR ## Consequences includes what changes for agents or developers as a result
